token_implementation.finding: All 192 tokens implemented as Figma Variables across 6 collections. No hardcoded values at the variable level. Collections cover colour (78), theming (58), typography (25), grid (5), borders (11), and spacing (15). Themes has Light/Dark modes. Typography has Mobile/Tablet/Desktop. Grid has 4 breakpoints.
observations: 22 text styles exist with hardcoded fontSize, lineHeight, and letterSpacing values. None are bound to Typography variables. All use Calibre font family. None have descriptions.
4 effect styles (sm/enabled, sm/hover, sm/pressed, sm/focused) have hardcoded shadow values. No variable bindings on any shadow property. Only 'sm' elevation level exists.
Typo in 4 text style names: 'Diplay' instead of 'Display' (Diplay Medium, Diplay Medium Emphasis, Diplay Small, Diplay Small Emphasis).
Zero descriptions on any text or effect style.

alias_chain_integrity.finding: All 58 Themes variables verified across both Light and Dark modes. 116 alias resolutions, 0 broken, 0 hardcoded. Every chain terminates at a Colors primitives variable. No orphaned aliases, no circular references.

token_architecture_depth.finding: Foundation provides two well-structured layers: primitives (Colors primitives, Spacing, Typography, Border size) and semantic (Themes). Semantic tokens alias primitives correctly. However, Foundation does not scaffold a component-level token layer. Spacing, Typography, and Borders are consumed directly as terminal values with no intermediate aliasing. The architecture supports two-layer resolution but not three.

primitive_naming.finding: Inconsistent across collections. Colours use value-based naming (color/primary/500). Typography uses scale-based naming (font/size/xl). Spacing uses bare numbers (4, 8, 12) with no namespace. Border size mixes stroke widths and radii in one collection. 'Colors primitives ' has a trailing space. Green colour scale has only green/500.
observations: Text style naming uses a Category/Name Variant pattern (Display/Display Large Emphasis) which is parseable but not connected to token names.
Effect style naming uses size/state pattern (sm/enabled) which is parseable but only one size level exists.

naming_convention_consistency.finding: Three naming conventions coexist across variable collections. Text styles add a fourth convention. Page names use emoji status markers. Foundation collection name 'Colors primitives ' has trailing space.

web_readiness_gap.finding: Typography has responsive modes (Mobile/Tablet/Desktop). Grid has 4 breakpoints. Spacing and border radius are single-mode. No elevation/shadow variables (only 4 effect styles with hardcoded values). No z-index tokens. No motion tokens. No platform overrides.

components.name: Slot Component
description_observations: Description is generic slot placeholder text, not specific to this design system.

components.name: Header Bottom Sheet
hardcoded_values: Bounding box x4, instance fills x2

components.name: Modal date picker
description_observations: Description references 'days/years' editing and 'variant properties'. Content appears original to this system.

components.name: Input date picker
description_observations: Description references field editing. Content appears original to this system.

components.name: .Building Blocks/Menu button
description_observations: Short description: 'Menu button'. Minimal but original.

components.name: .Building Blocks/Year
description_observations: Short description: 'Year cell'. Minimal but original.

components.name: .Building Blocks/Local M3 calendar cell
description_observations: Name contains 'M3' indicating Material Design 3 origin. Description: 'Day cell'.

components.name: Date and time - Collapsed
description_observations: Contains Apple Feedback Assistant URL: feedbackassistant.apple.com. Description text is 'Guidelines:' followed by empty lines and the Apple URL. This is copied from iOS design resources, not original to this system.

components.name: Switch
description_observations: Description: 'Switches are the preferred way to adjust settings. They're used to control binary options - think On/Off or True/False.' Uses Material Design language pattern ('used to control binary options'). Not a direct copy but echoes M3 phrasing.

alias_chain_integrity.finding: All sampled cross-library bindings resolve correctly to Foundation variables. Button, TextInputSolid, Alert, Checkbox, and other components trace through Themes to Colors primitives without breaks. Typography, Spacing, and Border bindings resolve to terminal Foundation values.

token_architecture_depth.finding: Components bind directly to Foundation semantic tokens and terminal-value collections. No component-level token collection exists. One empty local variable collection. No override point between semantic meaning and component binding.

component_to_token_binding.finding: 53 components inspected across 27 pages. Per-component binding scores range from 0 (TabBar, .ToggleButtonAction, Content card) to 4 (Radiobutton list, Checkbox list, Square card, TextAreaSolid, PaginationDots, Modal date picker, Input date picker, Date and time - Pickers, Date and time - Collapsed, ProgressBar, RiskIndicator, View switch). Mean score: 2.8. Recurring issue: 'Bounding box' rectangles with hardcoded grey fills (rgb(217,217,217)) appear in 18+ components.
observations: 3 components score 0/4: TabBar (entirely hardcoded), .ToggleButtonAction (entirely hardcoded), Content card (21 hardcoded values).
3 components score 1/4: Radio (only radius/strokes), CodeInput (only radius/fills), Toggle (only fills/strokes, knob hardcoded).
Radio has 12 variants with 6 interaction states but no fill bindings. It will not respond to theme changes.

component_description_coverage.finding: 8 of 53 inspected components have descriptions (15%). Of those 8: 1 contains an Apple Feedback Assistant URL (Date and time - Collapsed), 1 echoes Material Design language (Switch), 1 has a component name containing 'M3' (.Building Blocks/Local M3 calendar cell), 1 is generic slot placeholder text (Slot Component), 2 are short but original (Menu button, Year), 2 are substantive and original (Modal date picker, Input date picker).
observations: Date and time - Collapsed description contains: feedbackassistant.apple.com -- copied from iOS design resources.
Switch description echoes Material Design language: 'used to control binary options'.
.Building Blocks/Local M3 calendar cell has 'M3' in the component name, indicating Material Design 3 origin.
Only 2 of 53 components have substantive, original descriptions specific to this design system.

naming_convention_consistency.finding: Page naming uses consistent arrow prefix but 10 of 33 component pages have owner name suffixes (SASKA, SAB, SAS). Component naming is mostly PascalCase but inconsistent: ListItem vs 'List header' vs ListHeading. TextInputSolid has trailing space. Dot-prefix convention for internal components is used inconsistently (only .ToggleButtonAction and .Notification indicator use it; Content card, Radiobutton list, and other internal components do not).

web_readiness_gap.finding: All components designed at mobile dimensions (402px frames). No responsive variants. Mobile-native components with no web equivalent: TopAppBar, BottomAppBar, BottomSheet, Docked, Keypad. Missing web components: sidebar nav, top nav bar, breadcrumbs, table/data grid, tooltip, dropdown/select, modal/dialog, pagination.

checks_passed: Documentation template page exists in Component library
Changelog page exists in Component library
checks_failed: Page titles not free of owner names: 10 of 33 component pages have suffixes (SASKA, SAB, SAS)
Dot-prefix convention not consistently applied: only 1 component set uses dot prefix; multiple internal components lack it
Description coverage below 50%: 8 of 53 components (15%) have any description
observations: The documentation template page exists but does not appear to be applied to most component pages. Evidence: the template page is present while 85% of components have no description.
The changelog page exists but its content and recency were not auditable through the MCP (page content is frame-based, not text-queryable).
The definition of done is not enforced: Radio and Toggle are marked green (done) despite binding scores of 1/4.

data_gaps: ListItem (COMPONENT_SET, 20 variants) could not be inspected: page has 71 top-level children causing MCP timeouts on traversal. Retry failed.
.State Layer (COMPONENT_SET, 4 variants) on ListItem page: not reachable due to page size.
ListItem_Compact (COMPONENT_SET, 3 variants) on ListItem page: not reachable.
ListItem_Action (COMPONENT_SET, 2 variants) on ListItem page: not reachable.
.Supportive text, Text Left, Text Right, Risk indicator (standalone components on ListItem page): not reachable.
List header (standalone component on List header page): page returned no component sets on scan.
ListHeading (standalone component on List Heading page): page returned no component sets on scan.
Foundation text style variable bindings not checked at node level (styles exist as local styles, not applied via variables).
Foundation effect style shadow values not verified against any elevation token standard.
Changelog page content and recency not auditable (frame-based layout, not text-queryable via MCP).

dimensions_changed.reason: Methodological: v1.0 sampled 6 components; v1.2 inspected 53. The sample in v1.0 was biased towards weaker components (Radio, Toggle). Full inspection reveals the library average is higher than the sample suggested.
Added as dimension 9 in v1.2. Scored programmatically: 2 of 5 checks pass. The addition of governance at score 1 offsets the binding improvement, keeping the system average flat.

version_delta.narrative: The system score is unchanged at 2.1 (52% AI-ready) between v1.0 and v1.2. This is not because nothing changed. Component-to-token binding improved from 2.0 to 2.8 when measured across all 53 components rather than a 6-component sample. The v1.0 sample was biased towards weaker components. However, the addition of governance as a ninth dimension (scoring 1/4) offset this improvement. No remediation was performed between v1.0 and v1.2; this is a re-measurement with broader scope and an additional dimension. The flat score tells a real story: improved measurement methodology revealed better binding quality than initially estimated, but also revealed that the system lacks the governance structures to maintain or improve that quality over time.
